steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The prompt tells us the narrator describes Nana as planning for events like a human would. We need a quotation that directly shows Nana anticipating future needs — not just describing the Darlings' habits or Nana's emotions. Look for concrete, forward-thinking actions.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — "[Mrs. Darling] had found her two older children playing.": This is about Mrs. Darling finding the children, not Nana planning anything. ✗
  • Choice B — "Mrs. Darling loved to have everything just so, and Mr. Darling had a passion for being exactly like his neighbours.": This describes the parents' personalities, with no mention of Nana planning ahead. ✗
  • Choice C — "On John's [soccer] days [Nana] never once forgot his sweater, and she usually carried an umbrella in her mouth in case of rain.": Nana remembers John's sweater and preemptively brings an umbrella in case it rains — both are acts of forward-thinking planning, exactly like a human caregiver would do. ✓
  • Choice D — "Nana, who had been barking distressfully all the evening, was quiet now.": This shows Nana's emotional reaction, not planning for a future event. ✗
C is correct.
